communities.title: gRPC A/B Experiment Service Handlers and Metadata Enforcement
communities.summary: This cluster is the generated gRPC transport layer for experiment-related APIs, combining unary server handlers for `ABPlatform`, `AATesting`, and `Experiment` services with matching client stubs to invoke `Create`, `Update`, `Delete`, and query/selection RPCs. Each handler performs request deserialization, constructs `grpc.UnaryServerInfo`, and delegates to server methods through the optional interceptor chain. The `metadata` interceptor adds request metadata extraction into context with a required-metadata validation step, failing requests early with `InvalidArgument` when context metadata is missing.

communities.title: Account/Authorization Contracts and Service Behavior Tests for Feature-Flag and Promo Rules
communities.summary: This cluster spans identity and error contract models (`Role`, `Error`) plus Go rule execution/voucher preparation utilities used in promo decision workflows.

communities.title: Cross-service user, feature-flag, activity, and experiment orchestration
communities.summary: This cluster builds a service boundary that retrieves and normalizes user data from context/IDs/email/phone, translates gRPC responses into local DTOs, and enriches accounts with device and feature-flag state. It also integrates AB-test variant lookup to drive flag-driven behavior and includes activity handlers for creating/editing activity records plus loyalty completion checks. In parallel, it includes platform media upload handling for multipart file ingress, indicating a shared backend module coordinating user engagement, personalization, and media operations.

communities.title: Campaign Promotion Orchestration and Retry Queue Operations
communities.summary: This cluster appears to implement a cross-service promotional campaign flow, linking customer cart behavior (voucher validation, shipping-cost derivation, delivery-option output) with admin campaign execution paths that enqueue, retry, cancel, and reconcile campaign-related inventory discount jobs. It also provides supporting data and operational plumbing through activity-variable persistence (including Redis synchronization), campaign type metadata, bulk upload/error-reporting utilities, AB-testing attributes, and request interception to keep campaign state, targeting, and fault handling coordinated.

communities.title: AI Assistant Phase Execution and HTTP Provider Gateway Pipeline
communities.summary: This cluster defines the core AI chat orchestration flow, combining planning-execution logic with terminal escalation handling for unrecoverable errors in the cortex assistant phases. It also includes an AI gateway stack that constructs provider runtime/client configuration, normalizes and preprocesses requests, forwards HTTP calls, resolves provider metadata, and enforces read/timeout/validation behavior for safe request handling. The included Java helper method `InventoryDiscountService.copy` is a low-level stream-copy utility for transferring bytes between input and output streams.

communities.title: Activity Repository Testing Surface and Messaging/Utility Helpers
communities.summary: This cluster covers the `activity.repository` layer in the loyalty backend, including test scaffolding (`NewMock`) and a broad set of repository behaviors for creating, editing, and querying activity/customer records (coins, multipliers, validity windows, IDs, and notification/device lookups). It also includes `RandomUtil.generateRandomString`, a helper that constructs alphanumeric random tokens from random bytes for identifier-like values. Separately, `pubsub.Publish` is a Go publisher utility that sends messages with payload, attributes, and ordering key to Google Pub/Sub topics with debug logging.

communities.title: Cross-API Contract and Client-Service Layer for Accurate Order Operations
communities.summary: This cluster bundles API design artifacts spanning HTTP and service-contract layers, including a FastAPI REST template and Java domain models/enums used for voucher and dynamic landing-page sizing payloads. It also includes Accurate service symbols (e.g., `GetSalesInvoice`, `SalesOrderSave`, `VendorSave`, `Webhook`, plus test-oriented service entry points) that describe sales/invoice/vendor/order webhook workflows backed by protobuf definitions. Together, these pieces define a shared integration pattern for building and exercising external-facing APIs with standardized payload structures, method naming, and validation behavior.

communities.title: Marketing and Attribution Service API/Test Cluster for Activity Rules, Accounts, and External Integrations
communities.summary: This cluster groups HTTP-layer handler methods for activity-rule and activity-variable management (create, read, update, delete) plus shared response/context helpers used by service endpoints. It also contains unit tests and mock repositories for account detail retrieval, ad/asset scanning and inventory APIs, and attribution deep-link generation/editing, covering both happy paths and failure/edge cases. Overall, it focuses on API contract and external service interaction validation across related business domains.

communities.title: gRPC Service Boundary and Unit-Test Cluster for Accounts, AMS, Alert, and Anti-Abuse Modules
communities.summary: This cluster covers gRPC service wiring and test scaffolding across multiple Go modules, including generated client/server helpers, mock constructors, and a unary metadata interceptor used for cross-service calls. It also includes unit tests and helper constructors for core service flows such as customer/admin lookups, purchase/order creation, asset inventory status updates, alert sender retrieval, and blacklist email repository/service operations. Together, these symbols indicate a service-integration layer focused on validating external service interfaces and repository behaviors with mocked dependencies rather than runtime business logic itself.

communities.title: Cart and Campaign Pricing Orchestration for Checkout
communities.summary: This cluster centers on cart pricing and checkout-domain modeling, defining item and price-component types (including loyalty, PWP, discounts) plus cart utilities for weight adjustments, grouped price totals, and loyalty processing. It also wires in campaign/experiment and account-driven enrichment paths (customer lookups, campaign discounts, variant IDs, order request flags) used during order creation, supported by service-layer infrastructure such as auth clients, request interception, and resource/lifecycle helpers.

communities.title: ClickHouse Campaign & Keyword Analytics List Service Pipeline
communities.summary: This cluster builds and executes ClickHouse-backed list queries for campaign and keyword reporting, including request normalization, sorting, filtering, and cursor handling for paginated handlers. It maps rows from ClickHouse into API response objects, assembling campaign/keyword items with budgets, status, and derived metrics via shared metric conversion helpers and value extraction utilities. Supporting index builders and request/response helpers tie together service-layer criteria, query execution, and formatted output for both campaign and keyword endpoints.

communities.title: Inventory Stock Transfer and Bulk Upload Safety Cohesion
communities.summary: This cluster gathers domain helpers and service logic for inventory stock movement workflows, including unique-ID extraction, deduplication, and aggregate stock calculations that support transfer, adjustment, and schedule validation. It also defines transfer request predicates (e.g., rack, expiry-date, and transfer-all changes), status-role checks, and an in-transit rollback publisher used when stock adjustments fail. The accompanying tests exercise schedule comparison, SKU/asset limits, access-role rules, and positive/negative stock-trail creation scenarios for bulk upload/transfer operations.

communities.title: Product Catalog Intake, Registration, and Messaging Cluster
communities.summary: This cluster centers on WIMS product catalog domain logic for bulk updates and registration workflows, including bulk MSL row decoding/error tracking, product/category/packaging/rack/location lookup helpers, UOM classification and defaults, and computed registration business values (such as proposed margin). It also includes service-level hooks for seeding categories and emitting AIM full-update messages, while the included `astro-proto` setup indicates protobuf/gRPC message contracts are packaged and shared for integration across services.

communities.title: Purchase-Order Feature Gating and Inventory Replenishment Data Access Utilities
communities.summary: This cluster centers on two responsibilities: purchase-order feature control via `isManageExp` and a broad set of DAO/model helpers that fetch, de-duplicate, and aggregate inventory, movement, and product data by ID-based keys. The DAO functions build maps and sets from related entities such as racks, tasks, status notes, and purchase-inbound relationships to support replenishment and inbound workflow calculations. Together they form a data-composition layer for replenishment execution and PO management, including product/image/category enrichment for downstream business logic.

communities.title: Supply Order Stock Adjustment, Validation, and Rollback Coordination
communities.summary: This cluster wires together supply-order, inventory, and movement DAO methods that aggregate stock and quantity context (including rack-excluded availability, in-transit quantities, and mapped order/product/inventory relationships) used to decide and execute stock operations. It includes domain predicates and mappers for order state classification (direct, return, bad-stock), replenishment progress, and package/location lookups, plus stock-history/config translation to keep stock mutations consistent across workflows. It also covers rollback and integration paths by handling stock adjustment response parsing, emitting centralized IMS rollback events, and providing mock configuration access for supply-order constraints.

communities.title: Request Context, I/O Transport, and ID/Status Mapping Utilities
communities.summary: This cluster groups cross-cutting infrastructure helpers for moving data between system boundaries: HTTP request interceptors that propagate header metadata into request context, HTTP client wrappers that standardize outbound calls and JSON handling, and file helpers for upload/download and header injection. The DAO methods complement that boundary layer by extracting distinct order/item IDs and building status-note keyed lookup maps, enabling efficient reconciliation and filtering of purchase and supply order detail records.

communities.title: Background Processing and Area Lookup Infrastructure
communities.summary: This cluster combines three support concerns: protobuf service contracts for ERP job retry and WMS task operations, a small version-parsing utility, and a richer runtime layer for area data retrieval and background process lifecycle management. The dominant behavior appears to be infrastructure-oriented code that resolves area records for application use while coordinating long-running or periodic worker processes with startup, tracking, shutdown, and benchmark support.

communities.title: Cross-Service Commerce Domain Models and Knowledge Automation Utilities
communities.summary: This cluster mixes two concerns: lightweight automation scripts for maintaining internal knowledge artifacts and documentation, and a broad set of domain/entity definitions spread across Astro microservices. The service files model core commerce and operations concepts such as chat context, products, orders, inventory, promotions, loyalty, scheduling, observability, and external integrations, suggesting a shared backend platform where many bounded contexts exchange structured data while tooling keeps supporting docs, indexes, and sample outputs up to date.

communities.title: Ad Product Enrichment And Flexi Campaign Pricing Bridge
communities.summary: This cluster ties ad-scanning workflows to product-rich campaign data, with `admanager.adManagerService.ScanAds`, `ScanCampaigns`, `mapCampaignAd`, and `mapScanAdItem` pulling ad records and normalizing embedded product metadata such as variants, ROAS multipliers, and product status. It also includes the protobuf accessors that expose cart, pricing, discount, and product attributes consumed by `flexi_campaign.Handler.CalculateFlexiCampaign`, making this cluster a boundary between ad/catalog retrieval and downstream campaign pricing calculations.

communities.title: Authenticated Service Integration and Validation Helpers
communities.summary: This cluster combines low-level protobuf field accessors with service-layer glue used to build authenticated, cross-service workflows. The substantive logic centers on extracting the current user from request context, validating tokens and auth clients, shaping campaign payloads based on auth state, and performing customer or device fraud checks, while the many `Get*` methods are generated request/response accessors that support those flows.
